observations: Round-0 HIGH (entry 10's diff-pattern guard structurally blind to heredoc-internal behavioral changes) is CONFIRMED FIXED. Independently re-ran all 27 verification entries live against the real, current repository: confirmed the corrected expected pre-implementation pattern exactly (entries 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 FAIL -- entry 10 has correctly moved from PASS-now to FAIL-now, since it is now a 'becomes true' gate-dependent check rather than a 'stays true' invariant; the remaining 17 PASS). Independently confirmed entry 10 fails against the real, unmodified HEAD for the RIGHT reason (the byte-identity assertion itself, not the exactly-once FROM-string assertions, which passed silently -- confirming both pinned FROM strings genuinely occur exactly once in the real HEAD, i.e. the check's own precondition is satisfiable, not vacuously or accidentally unsatisfiable).

Reconstructed all THREE of this evaluator's own round-0 tampers, exactly as described in the round-0 review artifact, in a disposable scratch git clone (never the real repository), and re-ran the rebuilt entry 10 (extracted verbatim from the revision-1 contract JSON, never retyped) against each: (1) the narrowed NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex (dropping 'invalid'-token detection) now correctly FAILS. (2) the narrowed fixture glob (silently skipping all negative fixtures) now correctly FAILS. (3) THE CRITICAL ONE -- defanging the NDEBT-009 dormancy guard (`and False` appended to both firing conditions), which was invisible to the ENTIRE 27-entry rev0 suite including the sweep-green invariant -- now correctly FAILS. All three were applied on top of an already-compliant two-substitution base (i.e. each is a genuine 'mixed compliant+tamper' state), closing exactly the gap this evaluator's round-0 review demonstrated. The pre-implementation unmodified tree independently re-confirmed FAILS; the compliant two-substitution implementation (nothing else touched) independently re-confirmed PASSES.

Probed additional vectors beyond the three original tampers, per this round's dispatch: (a) an out-of-file-scope tamper (a stray edit to schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, a file entry 10 never reads) is correctly caught by entry 17's vlib_scope_guard, confirming behavioral-adjacent-but-out-of-declared-scope changes remain covered by a different layer, not a gap in entry 10 itself (entry 10 is only ever responsible for tools/validate.sh's own content). (b) whitespace-only drift (a trailing-space pair added to the shebang line, far from either pinned spot) correctly FAILS entry 10, confirming byte-identity is genuinely strict, not silently whitespace-normalized. (c) A NEW vector this evaluator constructed and disclosed for completeness: converting the ENTIRE working-tree file to CRLF line endings. This DOES pass entry 10 as currently written -- Python's default text-mode `open()` call (no `newline=''` argument) performs universal newline translation on read, silently converting `\r\n` back to `\n` in memory before the byte-comparison happens, so the check's own definition of 'byte-identical' is not actually comparing raw disk bytes for line-ending differences. However, independently verified this is NOT an undetected residual: the CRLF-corrupted script genuinely fails to execute (`tools/validate.sh: line 27: set: pipefail: invalid option name`, real exit code 2, since the embedded `\r` corrupts bash's `set -o pipefail` parsing), and entry 18 (the default-sweep exit-status-asserted invariant) independently re-confirmed to correctly FAIL against this exact tampered tree ('default sweep failed (rc=2)'). Unlike the round-0 dormancy-guard tamper (a small, easy-to-miss one-line change invisible to every layer), a whole-file CRLF conversion is also an enormous, immediately-obvious diff that Mode B's own whole-diff review would flag on sight, and it is independently, definitively caught by entry 18. Judged NON-BLOCKING: the overall 27-entry verification suite has no coverage gap for this vector (entry 18 closes it completely), though a cheap, purely mechanical improvement is available and worth noting for a future revision -- opening both the HEAD-show subprocess output and the working-tree file in a manner that does not perform newline translation (e.g. `newline=''` on the working-tree open(), and treating both as raw bytes) would make entry 10's own 'byte-identical' claim technically exact rather than relying on entry 18 as a backstop. Not required for approval, since no real gap in the overall verification suite was found.

Per-entry stability vs revision 0 independently confirmed: direct text comparison of entries 1-9 and 11-27 against this evaluator's own retained revision-0 record shows byte-identical commands and acceptance_test text throughout; only entry 10's command and acceptance_test text changed, exactly as disclosed. design_notes gained the new exact_transform_fix key and an updated head_caret_timing_note (now correctly stating entry 10 is no longer HEAD-caret-timing-deferred); scope, non_goals, and estimated_lines (13) are unchanged. Entry 16 (.agent immutability) was independently re-exercised in a fresh scratch clone as part of this round's own testing and remains sound in both directions (compliant state PASSES; unauthorized tracked edit to a different committed contract FAILS); no regression found.

Re-confirmed this evaluator's own round-0 trivial observation (R28-DUP's one-line citation offset): planning_notes_047.md is untouched by this contract (confirmed via git status/diff -- the file is not part of contract 047's scope of change), so the note is unaffected by this revision and remains exactly as characterized at round 0. On closer re-reading this round, the same 'line 1599' citation (vs the real line 1598) also appears verbatim in R3 itself (planning_notes_047.md line 154), not only in R28-DUP's cross-reference to it (line 304) -- R28-DUP inherited the offset from the reply it duplicates, rather than introducing a new, independent error. This remains a trivial, non-blocking citation nuance (the substantive claim -- validate.sh's exit code is literally the final `[ "${failed}" -eq 0 ]` expression -- is accurate regardless of the exact line number) and does not affect contract 047's testability or its verification entries in any way, since no verification entry depends on this citation. Flagging for the orchestrator's awareness: when posting R3 and R28-DUP as PR replies, consider correcting 'line 1599' to the real 'line 1598' in both, for precision -- this is a planning-note text edit outside contract 047's own diff, not a required fix to this contract.

Full untracked-aware git status check on the real repository, performed before, during, and after this round's review, shows no change attributable to this review beyond this artifact's own overwrite: only the planner's pre-existing .agent/feature_list_005.json edit, the pre-existing untracked .agent/contracts/047.json and .agent/planning_notes_047.md, and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md are present; git diff --stat HEAD against docs/planning/DEBT.md, tools/validate.sh, docs/planning/manifest.json, and .agent/run_state.json is empty. All fixture and adversarial-direction testing (including all five scratch clones used for the three tampers, the whitespace-drift probe, the CRLF probe, and the out-of-scope-file probe) was performed exclusively in disposable git clones under this evaluator's own session scratchpad, never a real repository path, with cwd explicitly verified before file operations. No git tag was created or deleted anywhere (real repository tag count unchanged at 10); no sign-off was simulated.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. The round-0 HIGH finding (entry 10's diff-pattern guard was structurally blind to changes made inside a check function's embedded heredoc body, for a file -- tools/validate.sh -- that ships inside the v0.7.0 consumer payload) is CONFIRMED GENUINELY CLOSED. All three of this evaluator's own round-0 tampers (narrowed NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex; narrowed fixture glob; and, critically, the defanged NDEBT-009 dormancy guard that was previously invisible to the entire 27-entry suite) were reconstructed exactly and re-run against the rebuilt entry 10, extracted verbatim from the revision-1 contract JSON: all three now correctly FAIL. The pre-implementation unmodified tree correctly FAILS; the compliant two-substitution implementation correctly PASSES; the exactly-once FROM-string assertions are independently confirmed satisfiable against the real HEAD. Further adversarial probing found no residual gap in the overall verification suite: an out-of-file-scope tamper is caught by entry 17's scope guard; whitespace-only drift correctly fails entry 10 (byte-identity is intentionally strict); and a newly-constructed whole-file CRLF-conversion vector, while it does technically bypass entry 10's own byte-comparison (due to Python's default universal-newline translation on read), is independently and completely caught by entry 18 (the CRLF-corrupted script genuinely fails to execute, rc=2) -- so no undetected residual exists at the level of the full 27-entry suite, unlike the round-0 dormancy-guard gap this revision closes. A trivial, non-blocking recommendation (adding newline='' to entry 10's file reads, for technical exactness independent of entry 18's backstop) is noted but not required. Per-entry stability is confirmed: entries 1-9 and 11-27 are byte-identical to revision 0; only entry 10 changed, exactly as disclosed; entry 16 shows no regression. This evaluator's one round-0 trivial observation (R28-DUP's 'line 1599' vs the real line 1598, also present verbatim in the R3 reply it duplicates) is unaffected by this revision (planning_notes_047.md is outside this contract's scope of change) and remains non-blocking, flagged only for the orchestrator's awareness when posting the PR replies. This contract is fit for implementation as-is. next_state: advance to Loop-2 (@generator implements the contracted scope; @validator Mode B verifies the implementation against this approved contract; this evaluator then runs the post-code QA verdict).
